title: гҒ„-AdjectiveгҒҸ (Noun)
description: A few гҒ„-adjectives that relate to time or location can be used like nouns in their гҒҸ form.

content.text: Table of Contents
The Basics

Using adjectives like nouns is common in many languages, including English. Just look at an expression like вҖң many will enter, few will win." Many and few are typically used as adjectives, and yet here they function as nouns.

In Japanese, a limited number of гҒ„-adjectives in their гҒҸ form can be used like nouns. These are adjectives that describe the characteristics of a time or place. The only addition to this is еӨҡпјҲгҒҠгҒҠпјүгҒҸ, which can behave as noun despite the fact that it doesn't describe a time or a place.
Patterns of Use
гҒ„-Adjectives Before Particles of Time or Location
All the гҒ„-adjectives in this group can be used before particles of time or location, such as гҒҫгҒ§ (until), гҒё (to), гҒ« (at/in/to) and гҒӢгӮү (from).

content.english: Next week, a friend from old times is coming to visit.

content.text: In the first example, еҸӨпјҲгҒөгӮӢпјүгҒҸ can be understood as вҖңold timesвҖқ or вҖңlong agoвҖқ and tells us the time period of the friendship, marked with гҒӢгӮү. In the second sentence, йҒ…пјҲгҒҠгҒқпјүгҒҸ means вҖңa late timeвҖқ, and the particle гҒҫгҒ§ tells us that it is the point in time that the action continued until. Though in English we can say "until late", in Japanese гҒҫгҒ§ can generally canвҖҷt be used with adjectives or adverbs.
гҒ„-Adjectives in Other Positions
Just three гҒ„-adjectives can be used in more of the ways nouns can be used. These are еӨҡпјҲгҒҠгҒҠпјүгҒҸ, йҒ пјҲгҒЁгҒҠпјүгҒҸ and иҝ‘пјҲгҒЎгҒӢпјүгҒҸ. They can be used as the subject of a sentence:
They can also be used as the object of a sentence:
Finally, they can be joined to other nouns with the particle гҒ®:

content.english: I went to a nearby store. (Literally: I went to the store of a nearby place)

content.text: This is a tricky one because it looks like itвҖҷs being used like an adjective, but since only nouns can generally be attached to other nouns with particle гҒ® , иҝ‘пјҲгҒЎгҒӢпјүгҒҸ is actually behaving as a noun here.
гҒ„-Adjectives that Can Behave like Nouns
The list of гҒ„-adjectives that can behave as nouns is so limited, that itвҖҷs worthwhile listing them here. They are organized by whether they are used to describe time, place, or other.
Time Adjectives
ж—©пјҲгҒҜгӮ„пјүгҒ„ (early) йҒ…пјҲгҒҠгҒқпјүгҒ„ (late) еҸӨпјҲгҒөгӮӢпјүгҒ„ (old) иӢҘпјҲгӮҸгҒӢпјүгҒ„ (young) е№јпјҲгҒҠгҒ•гҒӘпјүгҒ„ (childish)
Place Adjectives

content.japanese: ж·ұпјҲгҒөгҒӢпјүгҒ„ (deep) жө…пјҲгҒӮгҒ•пјүгҒ„ (shallow) й«ҳпјҲгҒҹгҒӢпјүгҒ„ (high) дҪҺпјҲгҒІгҒҸпјүгҒ„ (low) йҒ пјҲгҒЁгҒҠпјүгҒ„ (faraway) иҝ‘пјҲгҒЎгҒӢпјүгҒ„ (near)

content.text: ж·ұпјҲгҒөгҒӢпјүгҒ„ (deep) жө…пјҲгҒӮгҒ•пјүгҒ„ (shallow) й«ҳпјҲгҒҹгҒӢпјүгҒ„ (high) дҪҺпјҲгҒІгҒҸпјүгҒ„ (low) йҒ пјҲгҒЁгҒҠпјүгҒ„ (faraway) иҝ‘пјҲгҒЎгҒӢпјүгҒ„ (near)
Other Adjectives
Related Grammar

I went to a nearby store. (Literally: I went to the store of a nearby place)
